control_map_artifact.visual_assessment: Lineart diagnostic preserves strong hair, eye, face, collar, and blazer contours. It is high contrast and suitable as an inspected local lineart control-map input.

qa_findings: Generated image is a coherent hyperrealistic head-and-shoulders portrait with clean eye alignment, believable hair, stable face proportions, and realistic blazer/shirt clothing.
Lineart conditioning did not leak visible ink outlines or black contour stains into the face or clothing.
Background light strips are stylized but coherent and do not break the portrait.
Skin is slightly polished, but less problematic than a blocking artifact for first local smoke scope.
Hands, contact points, and full-body anatomy are not visible and are not certified by this sample.
remaining_risks: Single local smoke image is not full lineart lane certification.
Hands, full-body anatomy, contact points, and multi-seed robustness are not certified because they are not visible or not sampled.
Target-runtime EC2 object_info/path/hash/input proof, bounded generation, pullback, technical QA, and strict whole-image visual QA remain separate gates.
next_action: Update lane state and blockers to show the lineart lane is locally smoke-proven with notes, then choose either one QA-driven local follow-up or the next remaining ControlNet model proof.
